log_record.name: RCA_f179cd8b-c3af-f13f-a937-4efb9ff91010.txt
log_record.description: Source RCA log for CloudBees Job f179cd8b-c3af-f13f-a937-4efb9ff91010.

procedures.name: Build.Rel.602829
procedures.description: CloudBees procedure for project CRMBuilds associated with job Aurora.LA.3.1-00150-STD.PROD-2_20260706_233147_233154.

steps.name: VerifySrc
steps.description: Failed CloudBees step. StepId: f179cd8b-cf6f-f1bc-be4a-1ea035bf3243. Ran on agent host crm-ubuntu359 from 2026-07-07T06:33:38Z to 2026-07-07T06:34:50Z, duration about 72 seconds. Step exit code was 0; failure was detected by step logic rather than an OS-level crash.

step_classes.name: Source verification
step_classes.description: The VerifySrc step depends on reading source files from QCDFS-mounted shares for CRMBuilds source verification.

log_symptoms.name: CloudBees job completed_error / error
log_symptoms.description: Job f179cd8b-c3af-f13f-a937-4efb9ff91010 ended at 2026-07-07T06:38:05Z with status completed_error / error.

log_symptoms.name: Build outcome classified as Compile failure
log_symptoms.description: The build failure reason was classified as Compile, although the RCA states this was consistent with inaccessible source files rather than a code defect.

log_symptoms.name: VerifySrc step failed
log_symptoms.description: The VerifySrc step failed during the job window on crm-ubuntu359 between 06:33:38Z and 06:34:50Z.

log_symptoms.name: CIFS VFS SessSetup = -13 EACCES errors
log_symptoms.description: Datadog flex logs recorded 69,624 CIFS VFS Send error in SessSetup = -13 entries targeting \\QCDFS.qualcomm.com between 2026-07-07T06:00:00Z and 2026-07-07T07:29:59Z across 23 confirmed build hosts.

log_symptoms.name: Probable source file access failure
log_symptoms.description: The VerifySrc failure mode was described as consistent with source files not found or not accessible through QCDFS-mounted shares.

system_statuses.name: QCDFS authentication failures active during VerifySrc
system_statuses.description: QCDFS CIFS/SMB authentication failures began at or before 06:00:00Z, were active during the VerifySrc window of 06:33:38Z–06:34:50Z, and persisted until at least 07:29:59Z.

system_statuses.name: Widespread build farm CIFS impact
system_statuses.description: The CIFS SessSetup = -13 errors affected 23 distinct build hosts simultaneously, including fra-aci-ubld*, drekar-sd-*, and drekar-lv-* hosts. Top affected hosts listed were fra-aci-ubld429, fra-aci-ubld229, and fra-aci-ubld228.

system_statuses.name: SCM / Gerrit not implicated
system_statuses.description: Gerrit git-upload-pack operations for compile framework repos libbcc, slang, and mclinker completed successfully with status 0 during the VerifySrc window.

system_statuses.name: Commander not implicated
system_statuses.description: The RCA states there was no evidence of Commander-side failure and Commander was not implicated.

system_statuses.name: Infrastructure issue YES, code issue NO
system_statuses.description: The RCA assessed the incident as an infrastructure issue caused by a shared file server authentication outage, not a code issue or mixed code/infrastructure issue.

system_statuses.name: No formal Datadog incident declared
system_statuses.description: No formal Datadog Incident Management incident was declared for QCDFS or CRM during the 05:00Z–08:00Z investigation window despite the widespread CIFS authentication failures.

root_causes.name: Probable QCDFS CIFS authentication failure
root_causes.description: Probable root cause: a widespread CIFS/SMB authentication failure against \\QCDFS.qualcomm.com caused SMB session setup to fail with -13 EACCES, preventing access to CRMBuilds source paths required by VerifySrc. The RCA cites possible expired Kerberos ticket, NTLM credential issue, or server-side authentication service disruption as possible mechanisms, but does not confirm which mechanism occurred.

data_gaps.name: No Datadog telemetry for crm-ubuntu359
data_gaps.description: Agent host crm-ubuntu359 had no registered Datadog agent, no metrics, and no logs available, so the CIFS error was not directly observed on the failing host.

data_gaps.name: CloudBees step execution logs not accessible
data_gaps.description: CloudBees step execution logs from crm-ubuntu359 were not accessible for direct confirmation of the VerifySrc file access failure path.

data_gaps.name: Commander host not identified
data_gaps.description: The Commander host was not identified in the RCA. There was no evidence of Commander contribution to the failure.

data_gaps.name: No Splunk data queried
data_gaps.description: No Splunk data was queried during the investigation; Datadog flex logs were used for available coverage.

data_gaps.name: Specific authentication mechanism unconfirmed
data_gaps.description: The RCA identifies CIFS/SMB SessSetup EACCES failures but does not confirm whether the underlying cause was Kerberos keytab expiration, NTLM credential rotation, AD/LDAP disruption, or another authentication mechanism.

preventive_actions.name: Create QCDFS CIFS health monitoring
preventive_actions.description: Create a Datadog monitor for CIFS SessSetup = -13 errors on build hosts and alert when count exceeds a threshold within a 5-minute window.

preventive_actions.name: Implement Kerberos/NTLM credential lifecycle management
preventive_actions.description: Implement automated renewal of Kerberos keytabs and service account credentials used by build agents to mount QCDFS shares.

preventive_actions.name: Add pre-flight QCDFS connectivity check
preventive_actions.description: Add a CloudBees pre-step that validates QCDFS mount accessibility before starting VerifySrc and fails fast with a clear error message if the share is unavailable.

preventive_actions.name: Deploy Datadog agents to CloudBees build agents
preventive_actions.description: Ensure all CloudBees build agents, including crm-ubuntu359, have Datadog agents installed and reporting metrics and logs.

preventive_actions.name: Auto-create incidents for widespread CIFS authentication failures
preventive_actions.description: Implement auto-incident creation for widespread CIFS authentication failures; the QCDFS outage described in the RCA was not declared as a formal incident.

remediations.name: Investigate QCDFS authentication failure
remediations.description: Contact the QCDFS/storage team and arriverci.admins to determine what caused CIFS SessSetup = -13 errors starting at 06:00Z on 2026-07-07. Check for expired Kerberos keytabs, NTLM credential rotation, or AD/LDAP authentication service disruption.

remediations.name: Verify QCDFS mount on crm-ubuntu359
remediations.description: SSH to crm-ubuntu359 and run mount | grep qcdfs and ls /PRJ/qct/asw/crmbuilds to confirm the QCDFS share is now accessible.

remediations.name: Re-run failed CloudBees job
remediations.description: After QCDFS access is confirmed restored, re-trigger job f179cd8b-c3af-f13f-a937-4efb9ff91010 for procedure Build.Rel.602829 / Aurora.LA.3.1-00150-STD.PROD-2.

remediations.name: Review VerifySrc exit code logic
remediations.description: Investigate why VerifySrc exits with code 0 on a file access failure, because this may mask the true error and complicate diagnosis.

remediations.name: Install Datadog agent on crm-ubuntu359
remediations.description: Install the Datadog agent on crm-ubuntu359 to enable CPU, memory, disk, network, and log collection for future investigations.

annotations.name: Job context
annotations.description: Job ID f179cd8b-c3af-f13f-a937-4efb9ff91010; job name Aurora.LA.3.1-00150-STD.PROD-2_20260706_233147_233154; project CRMBuilds; agent host crm-ubuntu359; job start 06:31:54Z; job end 06:38:05Z.

annotations.name: QCDFS CRMBuilds paths
annotations.description: QCDFS hosted CRMBuilds-relevant source paths including /PRJ/qct/asw/crmbuilds, /crmbuilds/builds678, /crmbuilds/builds709, /crmbuilds/builds904, and /crmbuilds/builds907.

annotations.name: Primary evidence sources
annotations.description: Evidence sources listed in the RCA include CloudBees metadata, Datadog flex logs, Datadog metrics, Datadog infrastructure inventory, and Datadog incidents. No Splunk data was queried.

annotations.name: Confidence level MEDIUM
annotations.description: Confidence was assessed as MEDIUM because the QCDFS failure was strongly correlated with the job failure window, but crm-ubuntu359 lacked direct Datadog telemetry and CloudBees step execution logs were not accessible for direct confirmation.

annotations.name: QCDFS service owner information
annotations.description: Service owner listed for QCDFS: arriverci.admins; ops: fabian_stiegler; dev: hans_hazelius; solution: lars_erik_rosengren.

procedures.name: Build.Rel.602829
procedures.description: CloudBees procedure for project CRMBuilds. Job Kobuk.LE.3.1-00093-EBOOT.CPE.AB.WKK-1_20260706_232438_232456 failed within this procedure.

steps.name: VerifySrc
steps.description: Failed CloudBees step with stepId f179cc92-9c6b-f1c8-a6c8-2686f9881e6f. Ran on agent crm-ubuntulv39 from 06:27:19Z to 06:30:46Z for about 207 seconds. Process exit code was 0, but step verification logic detected failure.

step_classes.name: Source verification
step_classes.description: VerifySrc step class: validates access to source paths, including QCDFS-mounted source files.

step_classes.name: Compile failure label
step_classes.description: Build metadata classified the failure reason as Compile, although the RCA states this was a downstream misclassification of an infrastructure access failure.

log_symptoms.name: CloudBees job completed_error / error
log_symptoms.description: Job f179cd8b-c3af-f13f-a937-4efb9ff91010 ended at 2026-07-07T06:38:05Z with status completed_error / error.

log_symptoms.name: Build outcome classified as Compile failure
log_symptoms.description: The build failure reason was classified as Compile, although the RCA states this was consistent with inaccessible source files rather than a code defect.

log_symptoms.name: VerifySrc step failed
log_symptoms.description: The VerifySrc step failed during the job window on crm-ubuntu359 between 06:33:38Z and 06:34:50Z.

log_symptoms.name: CIFS VFS SessSetup = -13 EACCES errors
log_symptoms.description: Datadog flex logs recorded 69,624 CIFS VFS Send error in SessSetup = -13 entries targeting \\QCDFS.qualcomm.com between 2026-07-07T06:00:00Z and 2026-07-07T07:29:59Z across 23 confirmed build hosts.

log_symptoms.name: Probable source file access failure
log_symptoms.description: The VerifySrc failure mode was described as consistent with source files not found or not accessible through QCDFS-mounted shares.

system_statuses.name: Job status completed_error / error
system_statuses.description: CloudBees job f179cc92 ended with status completed_error / error at 06:35:12Z.

system_statuses.name: Farm-wide CRMBuilds infrastructure outage
system_statuses.description: RCA states the issue was systemic across the CRMBuilds build farm, affecting at least 19 hosts with CIFS errors and 24 hosts with Kerberos expiry events.

system_statuses.name: Outage pre-existed job start
system_statuses.description: CIFS failures were already active at 06:00Z, Kerberos expiry was recorded at 06:18:40Z, the job started at 06:24:56Z, and VerifySrc began at 06:27:19Z.

system_statuses.name: Infrastructure issue: YES
system_statuses.description: The RCA attributes the failure to shared Kerberos/QCDFS infrastructure rather than application code.

system_statuses.name: Code issue: NO
system_statuses.description: No compiler errors, syntax errors, missing symbols, or code-level failures were identified.

system_statuses.name: Commander not implicated
system_statuses.description: The RCA states there is no evidence of Commander-side failure or Commander contribution to this failure.

system_statuses.name: crm-ubuntulv39 lacks Datadog telemetry coverage
system_statuses.description: Agent host crm-ubuntulv39 was not registered in Datadog infrastructure and had no available CPU, memory, disk, network metrics, or logs.

root_causes.name: Probable QCDFS CIFS authentication failure
root_causes.description: Probable root cause: a widespread CIFS/SMB authentication failure against \\QCDFS.qualcomm.com caused SMB session setup to fail with -13 EACCES, preventing access to CRMBuilds source paths required by VerifySrc. The RCA cites possible expired Kerberos ticket, NTLM credential issue, or server-side authentication service disruption as possible mechanisms, but does not confirm which mechanism occurred.

data_gaps.name: No Datadog telemetry for crm-ubuntu359
data_gaps.description: Agent host crm-ubuntu359 had no registered Datadog agent, no metrics, and no logs available, so the CIFS error was not directly observed on the failing host.

data_gaps.name: CloudBees step execution logs not accessible
data_gaps.description: CloudBees step execution logs from crm-ubuntu359 were not accessible for direct confirmation of the VerifySrc file access failure path.

data_gaps.name: Commander host not identified
data_gaps.description: The Commander host was not identified in the RCA. There was no evidence of Commander contribution to the failure.

data_gaps.name: No Splunk data queried
data_gaps.description: No Splunk data was queried during the investigation; Datadog flex logs were used for available coverage.

data_gaps.name: Specific authentication mechanism unconfirmed
data_gaps.description: The RCA identifies CIFS/SMB SessSetup EACCES failures but does not confirm whether the underlying cause was Kerberos keytab expiration, NTLM credential rotation, AD/LDAP disruption, or another authentication mechanism.

preventive_actions.name: Automated Kerberos ticket renewal
preventive_actions.description: Deploy k5start, msktutil --auto-update, or a systemd timer on all build farm hosts to renew Kerberos TGTs before expiry, with renewal at 75% of ticket lifetime.

preventive_actions.name: Kerberos ticket expiry monitor
preventive_actions.description: Create a Datadog log monitor alerting on first occurrence of '[Errno 127] Key has expired' on any QCDFS mount path.

preventive_actions.name: CIFS SessSetup error rate monitor
preventive_actions.description: Create a Datadog log monitor alerting when CIFS SessSetup = -13 errors exceed 100 occurrences within 5 minutes across any build host, routed to arriverci.admins PagerDuty.

preventive_actions.name: Pre-flight QCDFS and Kerberos check in VerifySrc
preventive_actions.description: Add a CloudBees pre-step that runs klist to verify Kerberos ticket validity and tests QCDFS mount accessibility, failing fast with a clear QCDFS/Kerberos error.

preventive_actions.name: Fix VerifySrc exit code behavior
preventive_actions.description: Change VerifySrc to exit non-zero when source files are inaccessible so infrastructure failures are not misclassified as Compile failures.

preventive_actions.name: Auto-incident creation for farm-wide outages
preventive_actions.description: Implement automatic incident creation triggered by the CIFS error monitor, with assignment to arriverci.admins.

preventive_actions.name: Datadog agent deployment across all CRMBuilds agents
preventive_actions.description: Mandate Datadog agent installation on all CloudBees build agents in crm-ubuntu* and crm-ubuntulv* pools as a prerequisite for agent registration.

remediations.name: Renew Kerberos credentials across build farm
remediations.description: Run klist -e on all fra-aci-ubld*, lpi-aci-ubld*, crm-ubuntu*, and crm-ubuntulv* hosts to confirm ticket status, then run kinit or keytab-based renewal such as kinit -k -t /etc/krb5.keytab on affected hosts. Coordinate with arriverci.admins / fabian_stiegler.

remediations.name: Verify QCDFS mount restoration
remediations.description: After credential renewal, confirm access on representative hosts with ls /mnt/qcdfs/adas/SE03/DataLake/DCS02 and confirm CIFS SessSetup errors cease in Datadog logs with a target rate of 0 errors/min.

remediations.name: Audit CRMBuilds jobs in outage window
remediations.description: Identify all jobs that ran during the 06:00Z–07:30Z QCDFS outage and re-trigger any that failed with Compile / VerifySrc failures.

remediations.name: Re-run three confirmed failed jobs
remediations.description: Re-run f179cc92 Kobuk.LE.3.1-00093-EBOOT.CPE.AB.WKK-1, f179cca2 Kobuk.LE.3.1-00093-EBOOT.CPE.WKK-1, and f179cd8b Aurora.LA.3.1-00150-STD.PROD-2.

remediations.name: Install Datadog agents on affected CloudBees agents
remediations.description: Install Datadog agents on crm-ubuntulv39, crm-ubuntulv118, and crm-ubuntu359 to improve observability for future RCA investigations.

preventive_actions.name: Automated Kerberos ticket renewal
preventive_actions.description: Implement k5start, msktutil, or a systemd timer to automatically renew Kerberos TGTs on all build farm hosts before expiry, with renewal threshold at 80% of ticket lifetime.

preventive_actions.name: QCDFS mount health monitor
preventive_actions.description: Create a Datadog monitor alerting when CIFS SessSetup = -13 errors exceed 50 occurrences within 5 minutes across any build host, routed to arriverci.admins PagerDuty.

preventive_actions.name: Kerberos ticket expiry monitor
preventive_actions.description: Create a Datadog log monitor for [Errno 127] Key has expired on QCDFS mount paths and alert immediately on first occurrence.

preventive_actions.name: Pre-flight QCDFS connectivity check in VerifySrc
preventive_actions.description: Add a CloudBees pre-step validating QCDFS mount accessibility and Kerberos ticket validity before VerifySrc, failing fast with a clear error such as QCDFS mount unavailable — Kerberos ticket expired.

preventive_actions.name: Formal incident declaration for farm-wide outages
preventive_actions.description: Implement auto-incident creation triggered by the CIFS error monitor for farm-wide QCDFS Kerberos outages.

preventive_actions.name: Datadog agent deployment across all CRMBuilds agents
preventive_actions.description: Ensure all CloudBees build agents, including crm-ubuntulv* and crm-ubuntu* pools, have Datadog agents installed and reporting for future RCA investigations.

remediations.name: Renew Kerberos credentials on all build farm hosts
remediations.description: Run kinit or klist -e on all fra-aci-ubld*, lpi-aci-ubld*, and crm-ubuntu* / crm-ubuntulv* hosts to check ticket status and renew expired TGTs. Coordinate with arriverci.admins / fabian_stiegler.

remediations.name: Verify QCDFS mount accessibility
remediations.description: After credential renewal, verify on representative hosts with ls /mnt/qcdfs/ and mount | grep qcdfs, and confirm CIFS SessSetup errors cease in Datadog logs.

remediations.name: Re-run failed jobs
remediations.description: After QCDFS access is restored, re-trigger job f179cca2 — Kobuk.LE.3.1-00093-EBOOT.CPE.WKK-1 — and job f179cd8b — Aurora.LA.3.1-00150-STD.PROD-2.

remediations.name: Audit CRMBuilds jobs during outage window
remediations.description: Identify all CRMBuilds jobs that ran during the 06:00Z–07:30Z QCDFS outage window and re-trigger any that failed with Compile or VerifySrc failures.

remediations.name: Install Datadog agent on uncovered hosts
remediations.description: Install Datadog agent on crm-ubuntulv118 and crm-ubuntu359, both reported as having zero telemetry coverage.

preventive_actions.name: Implement Docker container cleanup before Make step
preventive_actions.description: Add a pre-execution check such as docker rm -f <container_name> 2>/dev/null || true before docker run to prevent name conflicts from prior failed runs.

preventive_actions.name: Implement agent memory monitoring thresholds
preventive_actions.description: Create a Datadog monitor alerting when system.mem.used on CRMBuildsLinux-Ubuntu18-04 pool agents exceeds 85% for more than 5 minutes, and prevent high-memory steps from dispatching to memory-pressured agents.

preventive_actions.name: Implement Docker resource limits
preventive_actions.description: Set explicit Docker --memory limits on Make step container invocations to prevent any single container from consuming all available host memory.

preventive_actions.name: Implement Docker daemon health checks
preventive_actions.description: Add a pre-step health check such as docker info or docker ps before attempting container creation; fail fast with a descriptive error if Docker is unresponsive.

preventive_actions.name: Enable agent-level log forwarding
preventive_actions.description: Forward Docker daemon logs, syslog, and CloudBees agent logs from san-w175-ma3-2-08 to Datadog or Splunk for future RCA coverage.

preventive_actions.name: Review concurrent job scheduling on agent
preventive_actions.description: Evaluate whether stepLimit=9 is appropriate for memory-heavy containerized builds. Consider reducing effective concurrent Docker container limits.

preventive_actions.name: Implement post-build Docker cleanup
preventive_actions.description: Ensure Docker containers, images, and volumes are pruned after each build step, for example with docker container prune -f and docker image prune -f.

annotations.description: Job started 2026-07-07 06:20:04 UTC. Make started 06:21:00 UTC. Make failed 06:24:01 UTC. Job ended 06:25:25 UTC with status completed_error.

annotations.name: Confidence level MEDIUM
annotations.description: The report assigns medium confidence because the shared dual-failure pattern indicates a shared cause, but the specific cause cannot be confirmed without host telemetry or step execution logs.

annotations.name: Dual-failure pattern analysis
annotations.description: If the cause is a code defect, both build variants would fail on any agent. If the cause is disk exhaustion, both agents would need to be affected. The report says the most likely explanation is a code defect, potentially compounded by disk pressure.

annotations.name: Sources used
annotations.description: Sources cited include CloudBees job metadata, Datadog logs, Datadog host inventory, Datadog pool disk metrics, Datadog monitors, Datadog events, Datadog incidents, Datadog APM spans, and cross-job correlation.

system_statuses.description: Active /local/mnt partition utilization alerts were present on hyd-e160-a05-3-06 and hyd-e160-a08-3-03. The RCA describes this as circumstantial infrastructure evidence rather than direct evidence for crm-ubuntu302.

root_causes.name: Probable CQ2390.LA.1.0-00317 code defect
root_causes.description: The report assesses a code defect in the CQ2390.LA.1.0-00317 codebase as the most likely explanation because two different build variants of the same codebase snapshot failed simultaneously on different agents with identical exit code and failure reason. This is not confirmed without step execution logs.

root_causes.name: Possible /local/mnt disk exhaustion on build workspace
root_causes.description: Pool-wide disk saturation on /local/mnt is a plausible infrastructure contributor. The report notes 13 of 22 sampled pool hosts at 100% utilization, but crm-ubuntu326 lacks telemetry, so saturation on the failing host is not directly confirmed.

root_causes.name: Compile failure during GNU Make step
root_causes.description: Primary failure mode reported: GNU Make failed with exit code 4 and CloudBees classified the failure as Compile. The underlying specific compiler error was not available in the RCA log.

data_gaps.name: CloudBees Make step output not available
data_gaps.description: The RCA states that CloudBees step execution logs must be examined directly to confirm compiler errors such as error:, undefined reference, No space left on device, or Killed.

data_gaps.name: No Datadog telemetry for crm-ubuntu326
data_gaps.description: crm-ubuntu326 has no Datadog metrics, logs, APM spans, or host inventory entry, preventing direct confirmation of CPU, memory, disk, or network state on the failing host.

data_gaps.name: No Datadog telemetry for sibling host crm-ubuntu302
data_gaps.description: The sibling failing agent crm-ubuntu302 has the same telemetry gap, preventing confirmation whether both failing agents were affected by disk saturation or another host-level issue.

data_gaps.name: Commander host not directly identified
data_gaps.description: The execution path inventory states the Commander host was not directly identified and no Commander-level evidence was found.

data_gaps.name: Specific SCM and dependency endpoints unavailable
data_gaps.description: The RCA lists invoked services as source code, compiler toolchain, and build dependencies, but states specific URLs and repositories were not available from metadata.

data_gaps.name: No Splunk index identified for CloudBees/CRM build logs
data_gaps.description: The available toolset did not identify a Splunk index for CloudBees or CRM build logs.

preventive_actions.name: Install Datadog Agent on crm-ubuntu302
preventive_actions.description: Deploy the Datadog Agent with system metrics and log collection because crm-ubuntu302 had zero observability coverage.

preventive_actions.name: Implement automated build workspace cleanup
preventive_actions.description: Add a pre-build or post-build step to purge stale object files and intermediate artifacts from /local/mnt on all CRM build agents.

preventive_actions.name: Add disk utilization alerting for crm-ubuntu302
preventive_actions.description: After Datadog Agent deployment, create a disk monitor matching the existing High /local/mnt partition utilization monitor pattern.

preventive_actions.name: Implement build agent health checks
preventive_actions.description: Before dispatching jobs to an agent, verify disk space, memory availability, and agent responsiveness.

preventive_actions.name: Stabilize ZooKeeper
preventive_actions.description: Investigate and resolve the root cause of the ZooKeeper daemon outage to prevent CloudBees Commander HA instability.

preventive_actions.name: Enforce build artifact retention policies
preventive_actions.description: Set maximum retention periods for build outputs on /local/mnt to prevent disk exhaustion across the build agent pool.

remediations.name: Retrieve CloudBees step execution logs
remediations.description: Access the Make step output from the CloudBees Commander UI for job f179cbf2-b44b-f18f-b2e3-1ea035bf3243 and look for compiler error messages such as error:, undefined reference, No space left on device, or Killed.

remediations.name: Check disk usage on crm-ubuntu302
remediations.description: SSH to crm-ubuntu302 and run df -h /local/mnt and df -h / to determine whether disk space contributed to the failure.

remediations.name: Check for OOM events on crm-ubuntu302
remediations.description: Run dmesg | grep -i "oom\|killed" on crm-ubuntu302 to determine whether the compiler was killed by the OOM killer.

remediations.name: Resolve /local/mnt disk full on build agents
remediations.description: Clean up build artifacts, object files, and intermediate outputs on hyd-e160-a05-3-06 and hyd-e160-a08-3-03, and apply the same cleanup to crm-ubuntu302.

remediations.name: Investigate ZooKeeper alert
remediations.description: Restore the CloudBees ZooKeeper daemon identified by monitor ID 115792998 to prevent job scheduling instability, while noting it is not confirmed as the direct cause.

remediations.name: Retry the build
remediations.description: After resolving disk issues, retry the build on a clean agent to determine whether the failure is transient infrastructure-related or persistent code-related.

root_causes.description: Primary failure mode reported: GNU Make failed with exit code 4 and CloudBees classified the failure as Compile. The underlying specific compiler error was not available in the RCA log.

data_gaps.name: CloudBees Make step output not available
data_gaps.description: The RCA states that CloudBees step execution logs must be examined directly to confirm compiler errors such as error:, undefined reference, No space left on device, or Killed.

data_gaps.name: No Datadog telemetry for crm-ubuntu326
data_gaps.description: crm-ubuntu326 has no Datadog metrics, logs, APM spans, or host inventory entry, preventing direct confirmation of CPU, memory, disk, or network state on the failing host.

data_gaps.name: No Datadog telemetry for sibling host crm-ubuntu302
data_gaps.description: The sibling failing agent crm-ubuntu302 has the same telemetry gap, preventing confirmation whether both failing agents were affected by disk saturation or another host-level issue.

data_gaps.name: Commander host not directly identified
data_gaps.description: The execution path inventory states the Commander host was not directly identified and no Commander-level evidence was found.

data_gaps.name: Specific SCM and dependency endpoints unavailable
data_gaps.description: The RCA lists invoked services as source code, compiler toolchain, and build dependencies, but states specific URLs and repositories were not available from metadata.

data_gaps.name: No Splunk index identified for CloudBees/CRM build logs
data_gaps.description: The available toolset did not identify a Splunk index for CloudBees or CRM build logs.

preventive_actions.name: Install Datadog Agent on crm-ubuntu322
preventive_actions.description: Install and configure the Datadog Agent on crm-ubuntu322 to collect CPU, memory, disk, and process metrics for future RCA investigations.

preventive_actions.name: Enable CloudBees log forwarding to Datadog or Splunk
preventive_actions.description: Configure CloudBees agent step logs to be forwarded to a centralized log platform so compile errors are searchable without direct host access.

preventive_actions.name: Add disk space monitoring
preventive_actions.description: Implement disk utilization alerts on all CRM build agents to detect disk exhaustion before it causes build failures.

preventive_actions.name: Standardize CRM build agent enrollment
preventive_actions.description: Audit all CRM build agents against the Datadog host inventory and ensure consistent observability coverage across the fleet.

preventive_actions.name: Pre-build environment validation
preventive_actions.description: Add a pre-step to the CloudBees procedure to validate toolchain availability, disk space, and required dependencies before invoking Make.

preventive_actions.name: JIRA tracking for build failures
preventive_actions.description: Ensure build failures are automatically linked to JIRA tickets for tracking and trend analysis.

remediations.description: Access the CloudBees agent workspace on crm-ubuntu322 and retrieve stdout/stderr output for the Make step, looking for compiler error messages such as error:, undefined reference, No rule to make target, or fatal error.

remediations.name: Check CloudBees job log archive
remediations.description: Use the CloudBees Commander UI for job f179cbb0-412f-f18b-a4a8-1ea035bf3243, Step "Make", View Log, to retrieve full step output.

remediations.name: Identify failing compilation unit
remediations.description: Use the Make output to identify the specific source file, module, or target that failed.

remediations.name: Review recent code changes
remediations.description: Review recent commits to the source tree used by build CQ2390.LA.1.0-00317-PERF.INT-1 for changes that may have introduced a compile error.

remediations.name: Verify toolchain availability
remediations.description: Confirm that the compiler, linker, and required build tools are present and correctly versioned on crm-ubuntu322.

remediations.name: Check disk space on crm-ubuntu322
remediations.description: Verify that the build workspace on crm-ubuntu322 has sufficient disk space, since Make can fail if the compiler cannot write output files.

log_record.name: RCA_f179c9da-d511-f1ad-b0ac-4efb9ff91010.txt
log_record.description: Root Cause Analysis report for CloudBees job f179c9da-d511-f1ad-b0ac-4efb9ff91010, build WLAN.TFH.3.6.c2-00721-QCATFSWPZ-1, outcome ERROR / ABORTED.

procedures.name: Build.Rel.602829
procedures.description: CloudBees procedure in project CRMBuilds for job f179c9da-d511-f1ad-b0ac-4efb9ff91010.

steps.name: Job Started
steps.description: At 2026-07-07 06:05:29 UTC, CRMBuilds / Build.Rel.602829 started on agent host crm-ubuntu221 with load already at 2.78.

steps.name: Massive Inbound Burst
steps.description: At approximately 06:07:04–06:07:16Z, about 97MB inbound was received in 8 seconds, with simultaneous outbound traffic beginning.

steps.name: IO Await Peak
steps.description: At approximately 06:07:40Z, system.io.await peaked at 70.67ms with IO util 27.47%, CPU iowait 3.55%, and load rising to 3.10.

steps.name: Sustained IO Contention Before VerifySrc
steps.description: From approximately 06:07:40Z to 06:09:14Z, IO await remained elevated while sustained outbound transfer continued; total outbound volume across the window was reported as about 555MB.

steps.name: VerifySrc Step Started
steps.description: At 2026-07-07 06:09:14 UTC, failed step VerifySrc started. IO await was still elevated at 10.65ms and load was 3.63–3.87.

steps.name: VerifySrc Ran Under Continuous IO Contention
steps.description: During 06:09:14–06:10:25Z, IO await values during the step were reported as 35.53ms, 11.24ms, 28.23ms, and 24.78ms; the step did not get a recovery window.

steps.name: VerifySrc Step Aborted
steps.description: At 2026-07-07 06:10:25 UTC, VerifySrc terminated with exit code 2 after approximately 71 seconds. CloudBees classified the failure reason as Aborted.

steps.name: Job Ended
steps.description: At 2026-07-07 06:18:14 UTC, the job ended with outcome error after extended cleanup following the failed VerifySrc step.

step_classes.name: Source Verification Step
step_classes.description: VerifySrc is described as the source verification step in which source verification and transfer activity occurred.

step_classes.name: Download Operation
step_classes.description: Pre-step setup included a massive source repository download of approximately 97MB inbound in 8 seconds.

step_classes.name: Upload Operation
step_classes.description: VerifySrc involved or coincided with a sustained outbound upload operation totaling approximately 555MB to an unknown remote endpoint.

log_symptoms.name: Exit Code 2
log_symptoms.description: CloudBees step metadata reported failedStep.exitCode = "2" for VerifySrc, interpreted in the RCA as a script-level abort, timeout, or transfer failure.

log_symptoms.name: Aborted Failure Reason
log_symptoms.description: CloudBees execution context reported executionContext.failedReason = "Aborted", indicating the step terminated before natural completion.

log_symptoms.name: IO Await Peak 70.67ms
log_symptoms.description: Datadog metrics for crm-ubuntu221 showed system.io.await = 70.67ms at approximately 06:07:40Z, the highest IO await across the analyzed session.

log_symptoms.name: Sustained Pre-Step IO Await
log_symptoms.description: IO await remained elevated before VerifySrc started, reported as 66.80ms, 31.16ms, 13.93ms, and 10.65ms from roughly 06:07:40Z to 06:09:14Z.

log_symptoms.name: Continuous IO Await During VerifySrc
log_symptoms.description: During VerifySrc, IO await reportedly never dropped below 11.24ms and reached 35.53ms, 28.23ms, and 24.78ms during the step window.

log_symptoms.name: Pre-Step IO Utilization 27–32%
log_symptoms.description: Datadog metrics showed IO utilization of 27.47–32.20% from approximately 06:07:40–06:08:40Z.

log_symptoms.name: CPU iowait Peak 3.55%
log_symptoms.description: Datadog metrics showed CPU iowait peaking at 3.55%, matching the period of the IO await spike.

log_symptoms.name: Inbound Burst About 97MB in 8 Seconds
log_symptoms.description: Datadog network metrics showed about 48MB plus 49MB received in 8 seconds around 06:07:04–06:07:16Z.

log_symptoms.name: Outbound Transfer About 555MB
log_symptoms.description: Datadog network metrics showed sustained outbound transfer totaling approximately 555MB, the largest outbound volume across the analyzed jobs.

log_symptoms.name: Chronic Host Load 2.78–3.87
log_symptoms.description: Datadog system.load.1 remained between 2.78 and 3.87 throughout the observation window, never dropping below 2.78.

log_symptoms.name: Cross-Job VerifySrc Abort Pattern
log_symptoms.description: The RCA identifies this as the fourth WLAN TFH VerifySrc abort with exit code 2 across the session, spanning three agents and two fleets.

system_statuses.name: Job Outcome Error / Aborted
system_statuses.description: Job f179c9da-d511-f1ad-b0ac-4efb9ff91010 completed with outcome completed_error / error and CloudBees failure reason Aborted.

system_statuses.name: Agent Host crm-ubuntu221
system_statuses.description: The job ran on crm-ubuntu221, GNU/Linux, with Datadog Agent 7.64.1 installed and metrics available.

system_statuses.name: Datadog Metrics Available
system_statuses.description: Datadog Agent 7.64.1 was installed and reporting host metrics for crm-ubuntu221.

system_statuses.name: No Datadog Log Pipeline
system_statuses.description: The RCA states there were zero log entries from crm-ubuntu221 because no log pipeline was configured, although metrics were available.

system_statuses.name: Disk Usage Low
system_statuses.description: Disk usage was reported as 9.34–9.43%, with no exhaustion risk.

system_statuses.name: Memory No Pressure
system_statuses.description: Memory was reported as approximately 192–193GB usable, with no memory pressure.

system_statuses.name: CPU Stolen Zero
system_statuses.description: Datadog metrics showed system.cpu.stolen = 0 throughout, excluding hypervisor CPU theft according to the RCA.

system_statuses.name: No Artifactory LDAP Errors
system_statuses.description: Datadog logs for the job window showed zero Artifactory LDAP failures, so LDAP authorization failure was excluded by the RCA.

system_statuses.name: No Active Incidents
system_statuses.description: Datadog incident review for 06:05:00–06:20:00Z showed zero active incidents.

system_statuses.name: Commander Host Not Identified
system_statuses.description: No Commander host was identified, and the RCA states there was no evidence of Commander contribution to the failure.

root_causes.name: Extreme IO Contention Caused VerifySrc Abort
root_causes.description: Primary probable root cause: a massive pre-step inbound burst of about 97MB in 8 seconds overwhelmed crm-ubuntu221 storage, causing IO await to spike to 70.67ms and remain elevated through the VerifySrc step; the VerifySrc upload operation then stalled under IO contention, exceeded a timeout threshold, and was aborted with exit code 2.

root_causes.name: Chronic Host Overload Contributed
root_causes.description: Contributing factor: load average stayed between 2.78 and 3.87 for the observation window, indicating concurrent workloads competing for IO and CPU resources and preventing storage recovery.

root_causes.name: Systemic WLAN TFH VerifySrc Abort Pattern
root_causes.description: The RCA reports a systemic pattern across four WLAN TFH VerifySrc jobs with exit code 2 and Aborted classification across three agents and two fleets, suggesting VerifySrc script or remote endpoint involvement beyond a single agent.

data_gaps.name: VerifySrc Step Logs Missing
data_gaps.description: Application-level VerifySrc stdout/stderr was not available in Datadog and must be retrieved directly from CloudBees to confirm the exact abort trigger.

data_gaps.name: No Application Logs Forwarded From crm-ubuntu221
data_gaps.description: Datadog Agent was installed and reporting metrics, but no log pipeline was configured for crm-ubuntu221.

data_gaps.name: Commander Host Not Identified
data_gaps.description: The RCA states the Commander host was not identified and no evidence was available for Commander contribution.

data_gaps.name: Remote Upload Endpoint Unknown
data_gaps.description: The sustained outbound transfer of approximately 555MB went to an unknown remote upload endpoint; destination and endpoint behavior were not confirmed.

data_gaps.name: Specific Abort Trigger Unconfirmed
data_gaps.description: The RCA assigns medium confidence to the specific abort trigger because step logs are required to confirm whether IO timeout, upload failure, or script-level detection caused the abort.

data_gaps.name: Remote Endpoint Contribution Unconfirmed
data_gaps.description: The RCA says the remote upload endpoint may have been slow or unavailable, but this cannot be confirmed without step logs or endpoint evidence.

preventive_actions.name: Configure Datadog Log Pipeline on crm-ubuntu221
preventive_actions.description: Configure log forwarding for crm-ubuntu221; the RCA notes this was the eighth consecutive job without log forwarding and calls for fleet-wide log pipeline configuration.

preventive_actions.name: Add IO Wait Monitor
preventive_actions.description: Create a Datadog monitor on system.io.await alerting above 20ms for CRM build agents so severe IO await can be detected before affected steps start.

preventive_actions.name: Reduce Concurrent Job Slots on crm-ubuntu221
preventive_actions.description: Reduce concurrent CloudBees job slots on crm-ubuntu221 to address chronic load and oversubscription.

preventive_actions.name: Upgrade crm-ubuntu Fleet Storage
preventive_actions.description: Migrate build workspaces to SSD or a dedicated high-performance NFS tier because the RCA states the crm-ubuntu storage subsystem cannot handle concurrent large downloads and uploads.

preventive_actions.name: Harden VerifySrc Script
preventive_actions.description: Implement retry logic, IO-aware throttling, and increased timeouts in the VerifySrc script for WLAN TFH builds.

preventive_actions.name: Add Pre-Step IO Gate
preventive_actions.description: Add a pre-step check that measures IO await and defers VerifySrc if IO await exceeds a threshold such as 10ms.

remediations.name: Retrieve VerifySrc Step Logs From CloudBees
remediations.description: Access job f179c9da-d511-f1ad-b0ac-4efb9ff91010, open step VerifySrc, and view logs to confirm the exact abort trigger and destination of the outbound transfer.

remediations.name: Investigate Concurrent Job Count on crm-ubuntu221
remediations.description: Check CloudBees agent configuration and concurrent job count for crm-ubuntu221 because load never dropped below 2.78.

remediations.name: Investigate Storage Device on crm-ubuntu221
remediations.description: Check whether the build workspace is on a spinning HDD or congested NFS mount; the RCA highlights 70.67ms IO await at 27–32% utilization as severe storage slowness.

remediations.name: Serialize Download and Upload Operations
remediations.description: Prevent the approximately 97MB download and approximately 555MB upload from occurring simultaneously; stagger operations to avoid the network-IO storm.

remediations.name: Re-run Build After Load and Storage Mitigation
remediations.description: Re-run the build after reducing concurrent job load on crm-ubuntu221 and confirming storage health.

annotations.name: Step and Job Duration

annotations.name: Confidence Level
annotations.description: The RCA states HIGH confidence for the infrastructure cause and MEDIUM confidence for the specific abort trigger pending step logs.

annotations.name: Infrastructure vs Code Assessment
annotations.description: The RCA assesses agent infrastructure issue as confirmed, remote endpoint issue as possible, code issue as unlikely, and mixed local IO plus possible remote endpoint contribution as possible.

annotations.name: Sources Used
annotations.description: Sources cited include CloudBees job payload, Datadog host inventory, Datadog metrics for IO, CPU, network, and load, Datadog logs for Artifactory LDAP, Datadog incidents, and cross-job analysis.

procedures.name: Build.Rel.602829
procedures.description: CloudBees procedure in project CRMBuilds for job f179c9da-d511-f1ad-b0ac-4efb9ff91010.

steps.name: Job Started
steps.description: At 2026-07-07 06:05:29 UTC, CRMBuilds / Build.Rel.602829 started on agent host crm-ubuntu221 with load already at 2.78.

steps.name: Massive Inbound Burst
steps.description: At approximately 06:07:04–06:07:16Z, about 97MB inbound was received in 8 seconds, with simultaneous outbound traffic beginning.

steps.name: IO Await Peak
steps.description: At approximately 06:07:40Z, system.io.await peaked at 70.67ms with IO util 27.47%, CPU iowait 3.55%, and load rising to 3.10.

steps.name: Sustained IO Contention Before VerifySrc
steps.description: From approximately 06:07:40Z to 06:09:14Z, IO await remained elevated while sustained outbound transfer continued; total outbound volume across the window was reported as about 555MB.

steps.name: VerifySrc Step Started
steps.description: At 2026-07-07 06:09:14 UTC, failed step VerifySrc started. IO await was still elevated at 10.65ms and load was 3.63–3.87.

steps.name: VerifySrc Ran Under Continuous IO Contention
steps.description: During 06:09:14–06:10:25Z, IO await values during the step were reported as 35.53ms, 11.24ms, 28.23ms, and 24.78ms; the step did not get a recovery window.

steps.name: VerifySrc Step Aborted
steps.description: At 2026-07-07 06:10:25 UTC, VerifySrc terminated with exit code 2 after approximately 71 seconds. CloudBees classified the failure reason as Aborted.

steps.name: Job Ended
steps.description: At 2026-07-07 06:18:14 UTC, the job ended with outcome error after extended cleanup following the failed VerifySrc step.

step_classes.name: Source Verification Step
step_classes.description: VerifySrc is described as the source verification step in which source verification and transfer activity occurred.

step_classes.name: Download Operation
step_classes.description: Pre-step setup included a massive source repository download of approximately 97MB inbound in 8 seconds.

step_classes.name: Upload Operation
step_classes.description: VerifySrc involved or coincided with a sustained outbound upload operation totaling approximately 555MB to an unknown remote endpoint.

log_symptoms.name: Exit Code 2
log_symptoms.description: CloudBees step metadata reported failedStep.exitCode = "2" for VerifySrc, interpreted in the RCA as a script-level abort, timeout, or transfer failure.

log_symptoms.name: Aborted Failure Reason
log_symptoms.description: CloudBees execution context reported executionContext.failedReason = "Aborted", indicating the step terminated before natural completion.

log_symptoms.name: IO Await Peak 70.67ms
log_symptoms.description: Datadog metrics for crm-ubuntu221 showed system.io.await = 70.67ms at approximately 06:07:40Z, the highest IO await across the analyzed session.

log_symptoms.name: Sustained Pre-Step IO Await
log_symptoms.description: IO await remained elevated before VerifySrc started, reported as 66.80ms, 31.16ms, 13.93ms, and 10.65ms from roughly 06:07:40Z to 06:09:14Z.

log_symptoms.name: Continuous IO Await During VerifySrc
log_symptoms.description: During VerifySrc, IO await reportedly never dropped below 11.24ms and reached 35.53ms, 28.23ms, and 24.78ms during the step window.

log_symptoms.name: Pre-Step IO Utilization 27–32%
log_symptoms.description: Datadog metrics showed IO utilization of 27.47–32.20% from approximately 06:07:40–06:08:40Z.

log_symptoms.name: CPU iowait Peak 3.55%
log_symptoms.description: Datadog metrics showed CPU iowait peaking at 3.55%, matching the period of the IO await spike.

log_symptoms.name: Inbound Burst About 97MB in 8 Seconds
log_symptoms.description: Datadog network metrics showed about 48MB plus 49MB received in 8 seconds around 06:07:04–06:07:16Z.

log_symptoms.name: Outbound Transfer About 555MB
log_symptoms.description: Datadog network metrics showed sustained outbound transfer totaling approximately 555MB, the largest outbound volume across the analyzed jobs.

log_symptoms.name: Chronic Host Load 2.78–3.87
log_symptoms.description: Datadog system.load.1 remained between 2.78 and 3.87 throughout the observation window, never dropping below 2.78.

log_symptoms.name: Cross-Job VerifySrc Abort Pattern
log_symptoms.description: The RCA identifies this as the fourth WLAN TFH VerifySrc abort with exit code 2 across the session, spanning three agents and two fleets.

system_statuses.name: No Datadog Log Pipeline
system_statuses.description: The RCA states there were zero log entries from crm-ubuntu221 because no log pipeline was configured, although metrics were available.

system_statuses.name: Disk Usage Low

system_statuses.description: Datadog logs for the job window showed zero Artifactory LDAP failures, so LDAP authorization failure was excluded by the RCA.

root_causes.name: Extreme IO Contention Caused VerifySrc Abort
root_causes.description: Primary probable root cause: a massive pre-step inbound burst of about 97MB in 8 seconds overwhelmed crm-ubuntu221 storage, causing IO await to spike to 70.67ms and remain elevated through the VerifySrc step; the VerifySrc upload operation then stalled under IO contention, exceeded a timeout threshold, and was aborted with exit code 2.

root_causes.name: Chronic Host Overload Contributed
root_causes.description: Contributing factor: load average stayed between 2.78 and 3.87 for the observation window, indicating concurrent workloads competing for IO and CPU resources and preventing storage recovery.

root_causes.name: Systemic WLAN TFH VerifySrc Abort Pattern
root_causes.description: The RCA reports a systemic pattern across four WLAN TFH VerifySrc jobs with exit code 2 and Aborted classification across three agents and two fleets, suggesting VerifySrc script or remote endpoint involvement beyond a single agent.

data_gaps.name: VerifySrc Step Logs Missing
data_gaps.description: Application-level VerifySrc stdout/stderr was not available in Datadog and must be retrieved directly from CloudBees to confirm the exact abort trigger.

data_gaps.name: No Application Logs Forwarded From crm-ubuntu221
data_gaps.description: Datadog Agent was installed and reporting metrics, but no log pipeline was configured for crm-ubuntu221.

data_gaps.name: Commander Host Not Identified
data_gaps.description: The RCA states the Commander host was not identified and no evidence was available for Commander contribution.

data_gaps.name: Remote Upload Endpoint Unknown
data_gaps.description: The sustained outbound transfer of approximately 555MB went to an unknown remote upload endpoint; destination and endpoint behavior were not confirmed.

data_gaps.name: Specific Abort Trigger Unconfirmed
data_gaps.description: The RCA assigns medium confidence to the specific abort trigger because step logs are required to confirm whether IO timeout, upload failure, or script-level detection caused the abort.

data_gaps.name: Remote Endpoint Contribution Unconfirmed
data_gaps.description: The RCA says the remote upload endpoint may have been slow or unavailable, but this cannot be confirmed without step logs or endpoint evidence.

preventive_actions.name: Configure Datadog Log Pipeline on crm-ubuntu221
preventive_actions.description: Configure log forwarding for crm-ubuntu221; the RCA notes this was the eighth consecutive job without log forwarding and calls for fleet-wide log pipeline configuration.

preventive_actions.name: Add IO Wait Monitor
preventive_actions.description: Create a Datadog monitor on system.io.await alerting above 20ms for CRM build agents so severe IO await can be detected before affected steps start.

preventive_actions.name: Reduce Concurrent Job Slots on crm-ubuntu221
preventive_actions.description: Reduce concurrent CloudBees job slots on crm-ubuntu221 to address chronic load and oversubscription.

preventive_actions.name: Upgrade crm-ubuntu Fleet Storage
preventive_actions.description: Migrate build workspaces to SSD or a dedicated high-performance NFS tier because the RCA states the crm-ubuntu storage subsystem cannot handle concurrent large downloads and uploads.

preventive_actions.name: Harden VerifySrc Script
preventive_actions.description: Implement retry logic, IO-aware throttling, and increased timeouts in the VerifySrc script for WLAN TFH builds.

preventive_actions.name: Add Pre-Step IO Gate
preventive_actions.description: Add a pre-step check that measures IO await and defers VerifySrc if IO await exceeds a threshold such as 10ms.

remediations.name: Retrieve VerifySrc Step Logs From CloudBees
remediations.description: Access job f179c9da-d511-f1ad-b0ac-4efb9ff91010, open step VerifySrc, and view logs to confirm the exact abort trigger and destination of the outbound transfer.

remediations.name: Investigate Concurrent Job Count on crm-ubuntu221
remediations.description: Check CloudBees agent configuration and concurrent job count for crm-ubuntu221 because load never dropped below 2.78.

remediations.name: Investigate Storage Device on crm-ubuntu221
remediations.description: Check whether the build workspace is on a spinning HDD or congested NFS mount; the RCA highlights 70.67ms IO await at 27–32% utilization as severe storage slowness.

remediations.name: Serialize Download and Upload Operations
remediations.description: Prevent the approximately 97MB download and approximately 555MB upload from occurring simultaneously; stagger operations to avoid the network-IO storm.

remediations.name: Re-run Build After Load and Storage Mitigation
remediations.description: Re-run the build after reducing concurrent job load on crm-ubuntu221 and confirming storage health.

root_causes.description: The RCA reports a systemic pattern across four WLAN TFH VerifySrc jobs with exit code 2 and Aborted classification across three agents and two fleets, suggesting VerifySrc script or remote endpoint involvement beyond a single agent.

data_gaps.name: VerifySrc Step Logs Missing
data_gaps.description: Application-level VerifySrc stdout/stderr was not available in Datadog and must be retrieved directly from CloudBees to confirm the exact abort trigger.

data_gaps.name: No Application Logs Forwarded From crm-ubuntu221
data_gaps.description: Datadog Agent was installed and reporting metrics, but no log pipeline was configured for crm-ubuntu221.

data_gaps.name: Commander Host Not Identified
data_gaps.description: The RCA states the Commander host was not identified and no evidence was available for Commander contribution.

data_gaps.name: Remote Upload Endpoint Unknown
data_gaps.description: The sustained outbound transfer of approximately 555MB went to an unknown remote upload endpoint; destination and endpoint behavior were not confirmed.

data_gaps.name: Specific Abort Trigger Unconfirmed
data_gaps.description: The RCA assigns medium confidence to the specific abort trigger because step logs are required to confirm whether IO timeout, upload failure, or script-level detection caused the abort.

data_gaps.name: Remote Endpoint Contribution Unconfirmed
data_gaps.description: The RCA says the remote upload endpoint may have been slow or unavailable, but this cannot be confirmed without step logs or endpoint evidence.

preventive_actions.name: Configure Datadog log pipeline on san-w175-ha1-4-07
preventive_actions.description: Configure forwarding of application and build logs from san-w175-ha1-4-07 to Datadog so future VerifySrc failures do not require manual CloudBees log retrieval.

preventive_actions.name: Monitor the VerifySrc upload endpoint
preventive_actions.description: Create a Datadog Synthetic test or monitor for the remote endpoint used by VerifySrc uploads and alert when unavailable.

preventive_actions.name: Add disk usage monitoring for san-w175 agents
preventive_actions.description: Create a Datadog monitor on system.disk.in_use for all san-w175 agents, with alerting at 70%, to reduce the risk of disk exhaustion.

preventive_actions.name: Enforce workspace cleanup between jobs
preventive_actions.description: Enforce CloudBees workspace cleanup between jobs on san-w175-ha1-4-07 because rapid disk fill-up suggests failed-job workspaces may not be cleaned.

preventive_actions.name: Review concurrent job limit on san-w175-ha1-4-07
preventive_actions.description: Investigate whether san-w175-ha1-4-07 should allow concurrent jobs, given the reported overlap between this job's teardown and the next job's startup.

preventive_actions.name: Increase VerifySrc upload timeout for WLAN TFH builds
preventive_actions.description: Increase the VerifySrc upload timeout for WLAN TFH builds, which involve large source trees and reported uploads in the 74–354MB range.

remediations.name: Retrieve VerifySrc step logs and identify remote endpoint
remediations.description: Retrieve step logs for the WLAN TFH VerifySrc abort jobs f179ca58, f179cb7a, and f179cb72, identify the common upload destination, and check its health and availability during 06:10–06:21Z on 2026-07-07.

remediations.name: Investigate disk fill-up on san-w175-ha1-4-07
remediations.description: Investigate why disk usage increased from 5.13% to 42.1% between this job and later job f179cb72, and verify workspace cleanup behavior.

remediations.name: Check overlapping job execution on san-w175-ha1-4-07
remediations.description: Verify whether CloudBees allowed concurrent jobs on san-w175-ha1-4-07 and whether overlap contributed to disk fill-up or later failures.

remediations.name: Re-run affected WLAN TFH VerifySrc jobs
remediations.description: After confirming the remote endpoint is healthy, re-run the three WLAN TFH VerifySrc abort jobs to determine whether they succeed.

remediations.name: Add retry logic to VerifySrc upload
remediations.description: Implement retry with exponential backoff for the VerifySrc upload operation so a single transient endpoint failure does not abort the entire build.

log_record.description: Source RCA log for CloudBees job f179cb7a-adf9-f1b3-9132-4efb9ff91010, build WLAN.TFH.3.7.1-01029-QCATFSWPZ-2, outcome ERROR / ABORTED.

procedures.name: Build.Rel.602829
procedures.description: CloudBees procedure for project CRMBuilds. Job f179cb7a-adf9-f1b3-9132-4efb9ff91010 was initiated under this procedure on agent host san-w175-ja4-6-06.

steps.name: VerifySrc
steps.description: Failed CloudBees step. Step ID f179cb7a-b96b-f157-a05c-2686f9881e6f. Started at 2026-07-07 06:19:31 UTC, aborted at 2026-07-07 06:20:36 UTC, duration approximately 1 minute 5 seconds, exit code 2.

step_classes.name: Source verification / source fetch
step_classes.description: Step class described in the RCA as source verification performing a large source package fetch and writing downloaded source data to local storage.

log_symptoms.name: Exit Code 2
log_symptoms.description: CloudBees step metadata reported failedStep.exitCode = "2" for VerifySrc, interpreted in the RCA as a script-level abort, timeout, or transfer failure.

log_symptoms.name: Aborted Failure Reason
log_symptoms.description: CloudBees execution context reported executionContext.failedReason = "Aborted", indicating the step terminated before natural completion.

log_symptoms.name: IO Await Peak 70.67ms
log_symptoms.description: Datadog metrics for crm-ubuntu221 showed system.io.await = 70.67ms at approximately 06:07:40Z, the highest IO await across the analyzed session.

log_symptoms.name: Sustained Pre-Step IO Await
log_symptoms.description: IO await remained elevated before VerifySrc started, reported as 66.80ms, 31.16ms, 13.93ms, and 10.65ms from roughly 06:07:40Z to 06:09:14Z.

log_symptoms.name: Continuous IO Await During VerifySrc
log_symptoms.description: During VerifySrc, IO await reportedly never dropped below 11.24ms and reached 35.53ms, 28.23ms, and 24.78ms during the step window.

log_symptoms.name: Pre-Step IO Utilization 27–32%
log_symptoms.description: Datadog metrics showed IO utilization of 27.47–32.20% from approximately 06:07:40–06:08:40Z.

log_symptoms.name: CPU iowait Peak 3.55%
log_symptoms.description: Datadog metrics showed CPU iowait peaking at 3.55%, matching the period of the IO await spike.

log_symptoms.name: Inbound Burst About 97MB in 8 Seconds
log_symptoms.description: Datadog network metrics showed about 48MB plus 49MB received in 8 seconds around 06:07:04–06:07:16Z.

log_symptoms.name: Outbound Transfer About 555MB
log_symptoms.description: Datadog network metrics showed sustained outbound transfer totaling approximately 555MB, the largest outbound volume across the analyzed jobs.

log_symptoms.name: Chronic Host Load 2.78–3.87
log_symptoms.description: Datadog system.load.1 remained between 2.78 and 3.87 throughout the observation window, never dropping below 2.78.

log_symptoms.name: Cross-Job VerifySrc Abort Pattern
log_symptoms.description: The RCA identifies this as the fourth WLAN TFH VerifySrc abort with exit code 2 across the session, spanning three agents and two fleets.

system_statuses.name: Agent host registered in Datadog
system_statuses.description: Agent host san-w175-ja4-6-06 was registered in Datadog with Datadog Agent 7.64.1 on GNU/Linux and had metric telemetry available.

system_statuses.name: CPU stolen was 0
system_statuses.description: Datadog metric system.cpu.stolen was 0 throughout the window, excluding hypervisor CPU theft as a contributing factor in the RCA.

system_statuses.name: Memory healthy
system_statuses.description: Memory was reported as 95.8% usable, not indicating memory pressure.

system_statuses.name: Disk capacity not exhausted
system_statuses.description: Disk usage was reported as 53.7% used, not indicating disk exhaustion.

system_statuses.name: Zero active incidents
system_statuses.description: Datadog incidents query for 06:17:00–06:25:00 UTC found zero active incidents.

system_statuses.name: No Artifactory LDAP failures in window
system_statuses.description: Datadog logs query found zero LDAP sync failures during the job window; the RCA excludes LDAP as a contributing factor.

root_causes.name: Extreme IO Contention Caused VerifySrc Abort
root_causes.description: Primary probable root cause: a massive pre-step inbound burst of about 97MB in 8 seconds overwhelmed crm-ubuntu221 storage, causing IO await to spike to 70.67ms and remain elevated through the VerifySrc step; the VerifySrc upload operation then stalled under IO contention, exceeded a timeout threshold, and was aborted with exit code 2.

root_causes.name: Chronic Host Overload Contributed
root_causes.description: Contributing factor: load average stayed between 2.78 and 3.87 for the observation window, indicating concurrent workloads competing for IO and CPU resources and preventing storage recovery.

root_causes.name: Systemic WLAN TFH VerifySrc Abort Pattern
root_causes.description: The RCA reports a systemic pattern across four WLAN TFH VerifySrc jobs with exit code 2 and Aborted classification across three agents and two fleets, suggesting VerifySrc script or remote endpoint involvement beyond a single agent.

data_gaps.name: VerifySrc Step Logs Missing
data_gaps.description: Application-level VerifySrc stdout/stderr was not available in Datadog and must be retrieved directly from CloudBees to confirm the exact abort trigger.

data_gaps.name: No Application Logs Forwarded From crm-ubuntu221
data_gaps.description: Datadog Agent was installed and reporting metrics, but no log pipeline was configured for crm-ubuntu221.

data_gaps.name: Commander Host Not Identified
data_gaps.description: The RCA states the Commander host was not identified and no evidence was available for Commander contribution.

data_gaps.name: Remote Upload Endpoint Unknown
data_gaps.description: The sustained outbound transfer of approximately 555MB went to an unknown remote upload endpoint; destination and endpoint behavior were not confirmed.

data_gaps.name: Specific Abort Trigger Unconfirmed
data_gaps.description: The RCA assigns medium confidence to the specific abort trigger because step logs are required to confirm whether IO timeout, upload failure, or script-level detection caused the abort.

data_gaps.name: Remote Endpoint Contribution Unconfirmed
data_gaps.description: The RCA says the remote upload endpoint may have been slow or unavailable, but this cannot be confirmed without step logs or endpoint evidence.

preventive_actions.name: Configure Datadog log pipeline on san-w175-ja4-6-06
preventive_actions.description: Configure log collection for CloudBees agent logs, syslog, and kernel messages so future RCAs include host and step log evidence.

preventive_actions.name: Add IO wait monitor
preventive_actions.description: Create a Datadog monitor on system.io.await for san-w175 build agents, alerting when IO await exceeds 20ms.

preventive_actions.name: Assess storage upgrade
preventive_actions.description: Evaluate whether the build workspace storage should be upgraded to SSD or a higher-performance NFS tier for IO-intensive WLAN firmware builds.

preventive_actions.name: Implement IO-aware concurrent job throttling
preventive_actions.description: Limit concurrent IO-intensive steps such as VerifySrc and Make on agents with spinning disk storage to reduce IO contention.

preventive_actions.name: Add pre-step IO health check
preventive_actions.description: Add a CloudBees pre-step that validates storage IO performance before large source fetches and aborts early with a clear error if IO performance is degraded.

preventive_actions.name: Increase VerifySrc timeout or add retry logic
preventive_actions.description: If logs confirm timeout as the abort trigger, increase the VerifySrc timeout for large WLAN source trees or implement retry logic with exponential backoff.

preventive_actions.name: Standardize san-w175 agent monitoring
preventive_actions.description: Audit san-w175 build agents for Datadog log pipeline configuration because the RCA states 20 san-w175 hosts are registered but appear not to forward logs.

remediations.name: Retrieve VerifySrc step logs from CloudBees
remediations.description: Access CloudBees Commander UI for job f179cb7a-adf9-f1b3-9132-4efb9ff91010, open step VerifySrc, and inspect logs for IO errors, timeout messages, or explicit abort signals.

remediations.name: Investigate storage device on san-w175-ja4-6-06
remediations.description: Check whether the build workspace is on local HDD, SSD, or NFS. Run iostat -x 1 or inspect /proc/diskstats to identify device type, latency, and health.

remediations.name: Check NFS mount issues
remediations.description: If the workspace is on NFS, check NFS server health and mount options, including rsize/wsize and async versus sync behavior.

remediations.name: Check concurrent job count on agent
remediations.description: Review CloudBees agent configuration and scheduling to determine how many concurrent jobs were allowed or running on san-w175-ja4-6-06.

remediations.name: Re-run the build with monitoring
remediations.description: If the storage issue was transient, re-trigger the build and monitor IO metrics during the re-run to confirm resolution.

annotations.name: Infrastructure versus code assessment
annotations.description: RCA classifies infrastructure issue as confirmed, code issue as unlikely, and mixed contribution possible only because concurrent workloads cannot be fully excluded.

steps.name: Setup
steps.description: Setup step ran from 07:30:33Z to 07:30:56Z, completed in 23 seconds with exit code 0. Environment variables, EcBuildSetup, and DB connections were initialized successfully.

steps.name: Make
steps.description: Failed step. Started around 07:35:00Z and invoked wlan_build_lnx_wkk.sh plus build.sh for target 9224.wlanfw.eval_v2 image=wlan_halphy with CLANG_VER=10.0.0, USES_FLAGS=ENABLE_CLANG, pgo_enable=true, cnss_chip_ver=v2, cnss_hw_version=e3r96, lto_enable=false, mac_num=1/2, ax_enable=true, disable_fbc=false. Failed around 08:17:27Z with non-zero exit code and errorHandling=abortJob.

root_causes.name: Persistent Compilation Failure in WLAN Firmware Build

steps.name: VerifySrc

system_statuses.name: Commander cluster healthy
system_statuses.description: Commander nodes las-colo12-j13-09, las-colo12-j13-10, and las-colo12-j13-11 responded normally. The NoSuchProperty response was a correct API response, not a Commander platform failure.

system_statuses.name: Code/build failure classification
system_statuses.description: The RCA assessed the failure as a code/build failure, not an infrastructure failure, based on BuildInfoJson failedReason=Compile and healthy infrastructure telemetry.

root_causes.name: Compile failure in Make step
root_causes.description: Primary root cause. The Make step, which executed common/build/build.sh for Hawi.LA.1.0, failed. The job's BuildInfoJson explicitly recorded status=Failed and failedReason=Compile.

root_causes.name: Missing SourceViolationRequired job property
root_causes.description: Secondary contributing cause. VerifySrc attempted to read /myJob/SourceViolationRequired, but the property did not exist on the job. Because VerifySrc used errorHandling=abortJob, the missing property independently triggered the abort chain.

data_gaps.name: Make step execution log not retrieved

data_gaps.name: Unknown source of missing SourceViolationRequired property
data_gaps.description: The RCA log does not identify which upstream step or procedure definition was responsible for setting /myJob/SourceViolationRequired, nor whether that upstream action failed silently, was skipped, or was not defined for this build type.

data_gaps.name: ARM license contribution not evidenced
data_gaps.description: The Setup command contained a comment about ARMLM_ONDEMAND_ACTIVATION, but the RCA log provides no direct evidence that ARM license acquisition contributed to the compile failure.

preventive_actions.name: Add pre-flight property check for VerifySrc
preventive_actions.description: Before VerifySrc runs, validate that required job properties, including SourceViolationRequired, are set. If missing, fail with a descriptive error rather than a generic NoSuchProperty abort.

preventive_actions.name: Improve Make failure reporting
preventive_actions.description: Configure the Make step post-processor to extract the compiler error, file, line, and error code into a job property such as /myJob/CompileError.

preventive_actions.name: Monitor SourceViolationRequired NoSuchProperty pattern
preventive_actions.description: Add a CloudBees monitor or Splunk alert for VerifySrc failures involving NoSuchProperty for SourceViolationRequired.

preventive_actions.name: Resolve ARM license workaround
preventive_actions.description: Engage the relevant team to resolve the ARMLM_ONDEMAND_ACTIVATION workaround noted in Setup, reducing risk of future license-related build failures.

preventive_actions.name: Validate associated image build compatibility
preventive_actions.description: Add a pre-build compatibility check for the dependency matrix of Hawi.LA.1.0-00631-PERF.AL-2 and its associated image builds.

remediations.name: Examine Make step log
remediations.description: Retrieve and inspect Make.f1792a35-bafc-f101-985d-1ea035bf3243.log from crm_lv on crm-ubuntulv02 or the CloudBees job log archive to identify the exact compiler error, source file, line number, and failing component.

remediations.name: Investigate SourceViolationRequired property population
remediations.description: Determine which upstream step should set /myJob/SourceViolationRequired. Review Setup, Init, and SyncSetup logs and the Build.Rel.602829 procedure definition for the PERF.AL client and Hawi.LA.1.0 image.

remediations.name: Update VerifySrc missing-property handling
remediations.description: Ensure SourceViolationRequired is always set before VerifySrc, or update VerifySrc to treat an absent property as a safe default such as false when appropriate.

remediations.name: Fix compile error and rerun build
remediations.description: After identifying the failing compilation component from the Make log, fix the source code or build configuration and rerun the build.

annotations.name: High confidence
annotations.description: The RCA states overall confidence is high because BuildInfoJson directly records failedReason=Compile and Commander raw XML directly records the NoSuchProperty error.

annotations.name: Parallel failure context
annotations.description: Make, VerifySrc, and ConcurrentCopy were dispatched simultaneously at 04:03:45 PDT. Make and VerifySrc both failed with errorHandling=abortJob.

annotations.name: SaveBuild not independent root cause
annotations.description: SaveBuild failed after Make and VerifySrc had already triggered abort signals; the RCA classifies SaveBuild as a cascaded failure, not an independent root cause.

annotations.name: Post-job NoSuchProperty errors out of scope
annotations.description: NoSuchProperty errors for report-urls and customType occurred around 06:29:57 PDT, after job completion, and were marked out of scope for the RCA.

root_causes.name: IO contention during compilation caused by network-IO storm
root_causes.description: The RCA identifies the probable root cause as severe IO contention during the Make step. Large concurrent network transfers, approximately 91MB inbound and approximately 414MB outbound, competed with compiler reads and object-file writes, causing IO await to reach 10.84ms, IO utilization 17.2%, CPU iowait 3.63%, and system load 4.74 before Make exited with code 4.

data_gaps.name: Exact compiler error missing
data_gaps.description: The exact compiler error message, such as missing symbol, failed target, missing file, read error, undefined symbol, compiler timeout, or IO error, is not available in Datadog and must be retrieved from CloudBees step logs.

data_gaps.name: No application-level logs forwarded
data_gaps.description: The Datadog Agent is installed on san-w175-ma3-2-05 and reports metrics, but no log pipeline is configured, so application-level logs and compiler output are absent from Datadog.

data_gaps.name: Outbound transfer process not identified
data_gaps.description: The RCA reports approximately 414MB outbound during compilation but does not identify the process responsible for the outbound transfer.

data_gaps.name: Commander host not identified
data_gaps.description: The RCA states that no Commander host was identified and there is no evidence of Commander contribution to the failure.

data_gaps.name: Code defect cannot be excluded
data_gaps.description: The RCA states that a pre-existing code issue in the Snapdragon Auto source tree cannot be excluded without CloudBees Make step logs.

preventive_actions.name: Configure Datadog Log Pipeline on san-w175-ma3-2-05
preventive_actions.description: Configure log collection for CloudBees agent logs, compiler output, and syslog because the agent currently reports metrics but no application-level logs.

preventive_actions.name: Add IO wait monitor for san-w175 build agents
preventive_actions.description: Create a Datadog monitor on system.io.await alerting above 10ms for san-w175 agents.

preventive_actions.name: Serialize network and compile operations in Build.Rel.602829
preventive_actions.description: Modify the CloudBees procedure so large source downloads and artifact uploads complete before the Make step begins, avoiding concurrent network IO during compilation.

preventive_actions.name: Storage upgrade assessment for san-w175-ma3 rack
preventive_actions.description: Evaluate whether san-w175 build workspace storage should be upgraded to SSD or a higher-performance NFS tier.

preventive_actions.name: Concurrent job throttling
preventive_actions.description: Limit concurrent IO-intensive jobs on san-w175-ma3-2-05 to reduce risk of network-IO storms during compilation.

preventive_actions.name: Add pre-Make IO health check
preventive_actions.description: Add a CloudBees pre-step that checks storage IO performance before invoking Make and delays the Make step if IO await exceeds a threshold such as 5ms.

remediations.name: Retrieve Make step logs from CloudBees
remediations.description: Access the CloudBees Commander UI for job f179c9e2-7f0b-f109-a3ba-1ea035bf3243, open the Make step log, and identify the specific compiler error that caused exit code 4.

remediations.name: Investigate large outbound transfer during compilation
remediations.description: Identify what process sent approximately 414MB outbound during the Make step and prevent it from running simultaneously with active compilation.

remediations.name: Investigate storage device on san-w175-ma3-2-05
remediations.description: Check whether the build workspace is on a spinning HDD or congested NFS mount and consider migration to SSD or a high-performance NFS tier for Snapdragon Auto builds.

remediations.name: Separate network-intensive operations from compilation
remediations.description: Ensure VerifySrc/upload or other network-intensive phases complete before Make begins so compiler IO does not contend with large network buffer flushes.

remediations.name: Re-run the build
remediations.description: Re-trigger the build if the IO contention was caused by a concurrent job or transient network burst, preferably with network-intensive operations serialized before Make.

annotations.name: Confidence MEDIUM-HIGH

annotations.name: Code issue possible but unconfirmed
