log_symptoms.description: CloudBees step metadata reported failedStep.exitCode = "2". The report interprets this as a script-level abort or return code from a failed transfer utility.

log_symptoms.name: Declared failure reason Aborted
log_symptoms.description: CloudBees executionContext.failedReason = "Aborted", indicating the step terminated before natural completion.

log_symptoms.name: Pre-step IO contention
log_symptoms.description: Datadog metrics showed system.io.await peaking at 15.97ms and system.io.util at 15.97% around 2026-07-07 06:19:23 UTC, about 27 seconds before VerifySrc started.

log_symptoms.name: Massive outbound network burst
log_symptoms.description: Datadog system.net.bytes_sent showed about 354MB outbound during the step window in bursts of approximately 55MB, 40MB, 54MB, 107MB, and 98MB.

log_symptoms.name: Minimal inbound traffic
log_symptoms.description: During the step, inbound traffic was only about 1.2–1.5MB, with no large source-fetch download pattern observed.

log_symptoms.name: Rising system load
log_symptoms.description: Datadog system.load.1 rose from 0.81 to 1.97 across the correlation window, consistent with accumulating concurrent process pressure.

log_symptoms.name: Abrupt CPU user drop
log_symptoms.description: Datadog system.cpu.user dropped from about 1.02% to 0.07% near the end of the step, consistent with process termination.

system_statuses.name: Job outcome completed_error / error
system_statuses.description: The job ended with outcome completed_error / error after the VerifySrc step aborted.

system_statuses.name: Datadog Agent installed and reporting metrics
system_statuses.description: Agent host san-w175-ha1-4-07 is GNU/Linux with Datadog Agent 7.64.1 installed, and host metrics were available.

system_statuses.name: CPU stolen equals zero
system_statuses.description: Datadog system.cpu.stolen was 0 throughout the correlation window, excluding hypervisor CPU theft as a contributing factor.

system_statuses.name: Memory and disk not exhausted
system_statuses.description: Memory was reported as 392–393GB usable and disk usage as 42.1%, so memory pressure and disk exhaustion were excluded by the report.

system_statuses.name: Zero active incidents
system_statuses.description: Datadog incidents showed zero active incidents during the job execution window.

system_statuses.name: Zero Artifactory LDAP failures
system_statuses.description: Datadog logs for Artifactory showed zero LDAP sync failures during the job window; LDAP was excluded as a contributing factor.

root_causes.name: Probable outbound transfer timeout or abort under pre-existing IO pressure
root_causes.description: The report identifies the probable root cause as the VerifySrc step initiating a large outbound transfer of about 354MB while the agent storage subsystem was already under IO contention from a concurrent workload. The upload may have stalled, timed out, or been rejected/dropped by the remote endpoint; VerifySrc then exited with code 2 and CloudBees classified the step as Aborted.

data_gaps.name: No application-level logs forwarded to Datadog
data_gaps.description: The Datadog Agent was installed and reporting metrics, but no log pipeline was configured for san-w175-ha1-4-07, resulting in zero log entries from the host.

data_gaps.name: VerifySrc stdout/stderr not available in supplied evidence
data_gaps.description: The report states that step-level stdout/stderr must be retrieved directly from CloudBees to confirm the exact abort trigger.

data_gaps.name: Destination of outbound transfer unknown
data_gaps.description: The remote endpoint receiving the approximately 354MB outbound transfer could not be identified from available metadata. Candidate endpoint types mentioned were Artifactory, SCM, or build coordination server, but no specific endpoint was evidenced.

data_gaps.name: Exact abort trigger unknown
data_gaps.description: The report could not determine whether the final abort was caused by timeout, connection reset, remote rejection, or another transfer failure without step logs.

data_gaps.name: Concurrent workload identity unknown
data_gaps.description: Rising load and pre-step IO contention suggested at least one other concurrent process or job, but the specific workload was not identified in the supplied evidence.

data_gaps.name: Commander host not identified
data_gaps.description: No Commander host was identified in job metadata, and there was no evidence of Commander contribution to the failure.

data_gaps.name: Remote upload endpoint telemetry unavailable
data_gaps.description: The remote endpoint was in the execution path, but endpoint identity and telemetry were unavailable in the supplied evidence.

preventive_actions.name: Configure Datadog log pipeline
preventive_actions.description: Configure log collection for CloudBees agent logs and syslog on san-w175-ha1-4-07. The report recommends fleet-wide remediation for all san-w175-ha1 hosts that lack log pipelines.

preventive_actions.name: Add IO wait and network saturation monitors
preventive_actions.description: Create Datadog monitors for system.io.await, alerting above 20ms, and system.net.bytes_sent for sustained high outbound traffic across san-w175 build agents.

preventive_actions.name: Concurrent job throttling
preventive_actions.description: Implement IO-aware job scheduling and limit concurrent IO-intensive steps on the same agent to reduce storage contention.

preventive_actions.name: Add upload retry logic in VerifySrc
preventive_actions.description: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for the outbound transfer in the VerifySrc script so a transient network stall does not abort the entire build.

preventive_actions.name: Increase or improve upload timeout behavior
preventive_actions.description: If the abort is caused by a fixed timeout, increase the timeout for large WLAN source tree uploads or implement chunked transfer with progress monitoring.

preventive_actions.name: Perform cross-job pattern analysis
preventive_actions.description: Investigate whether VerifySrc has a systemic timeout or transfer issue across WLAN TFH builds, because this was reported as the second consecutive WLAN TFH VerifySrc abort with exit code 2 and failure reason Aborted across different agents.

preventive_actions.name: Standardize agent log forwarding
preventive_actions.description: Audit san-w175 build agents across ha1 and ja4 racks for Datadog log pipeline configuration and standardize log forwarding across the fleet.

remediations.name: Retrieve VerifySrc step logs from CloudBees
remediations.description: Use the CloudBees Commander UI for job f179cb72-2009-f120-bf77-2686f9881e6f, open Step VerifySrc, and view the log to identify the outbound transfer destination and exact error message.

remediations.name: Identify concurrent workload causing IO contention
remediations.description: Check CloudBees agent configuration and job activity on san-w175-ha1-4-07 around 2026-07-07 06:19:14–06:19:32 UTC to determine whether other jobs were running IO-intensive operations.

remediations.name: Investigate upload endpoint
remediations.description: After identifying the outbound transfer destination from step logs, check health and availability of that endpoint during 2026-07-07 06:19:50–06:21:07 UTC.

remediations.name: Check network bandwidth on san-w175-ha1-4-07
remediations.description: Verify whether the agent network interface was saturated or competing with traffic from concurrent jobs during the approximately 354MB upload in about 45 seconds.

remediations.name: Re-run the build
remediations.description: If IO contention and upload stall were transient, re-trigger the build and monitor IO and network metrics during the re-run.

procedures.name: Build.Rel.602829
procedures.description: CloudBees procedure for CRMBuilds job f179cba9-dcae-f125-a110-2686f9881e6f.

steps.name: VerifySrc
steps.description: Failed CloudBees step with Step ID f179cba9-e847-f173-8c29-1ea035bf3243, started at 2026-07-07 06:19:21 UTC on agent host crm-ubuntu327 and failed at 06:21:12 UTC with exit code 0.

steps.name: Job Started
steps.description: CRMBuilds / Build.Rel.602829 job initiated at 2026-07-07 06:18:26 UTC.

steps.name: Job Ended
steps.description: Job ended at 2026-07-07 06:24:13 UTC with outcome error.

step_classes.name: Source verification
step_classes.description: VerifySrc is described as a source verification step, likely fetching or validating source artifacts from Artifactory.

step_classes.name: Compile failure classification
step_classes.description: CloudBees executionContext classified the failure reason as Compile, although the report notes this may be a broad classification applied when source verification fails before compilation begins.

log_symptoms.name: Job outcome error
log_symptoms.description: CloudBees job f179cba9-dcae-f125-a110-2686f9881e6f completed with completed_error / error outcome.

log_symptoms.name: VerifySrc failed with exit code 0
log_symptoms.description: VerifySrc was marked failed even though failedStep.exitCode was 0, indicating the process exited cleanly while validation output indicated failure.

log_symptoms.name: Artifactory LDAP sync failure for Restricted-CRMBuilds-Read-Only
log_symptoms.description: Artifactory logs showed 'Could not sync ldap groups Restricted-CRMBuilds-Read-Only: qed-ldap.qualcomm.com:636' with 115 occurrences in the 06:00 UTC hour and first burst at 2026-07-07 06:19:14Z.

log_symptoms.name: Acute LDAP failure pattern
log_symptoms.description: The report states there were zero LDAP sync failures in the 24 hours preceding the 06:00 UTC hour, making the failure acute rather than chronic.

log_symptoms.name: Fleet-wide Artifactory ent-las impact
log_symptoms.description: LDAP sync failures affected artifactory-ent-las-04, -05, -06, -07, -08, -09, and -10.

log_symptoms.name: No active Datadog incidents during job window
log_symptoms.description: Datadog incidents query found zero active incidents during the job execution window.

log_symptoms.name: No Datadog monitors for crm-ubuntu327
log_symptoms.description: Datadog monitor inventory found no monitors configured for crm-ubuntu327.

system_statuses.name: CloudBees job completed_error
system_statuses.description: Job outcome was completed_error / error.

system_statuses.name: crm-ubuntu327 absent from Datadog telemetry
system_statuses.description: The agent host crm-ubuntu327 was absent from Datadog host inventory; no CPU, memory, disk, network metrics, or logs were available.

system_statuses.name: Artifactory LDAP group sync degraded
system_statuses.description: Artifactory ent-las nodes were unable to sync the Restricted-CRMBuilds-Read-Only LDAP group from qed-ldap.qualcomm.com:636 during the build window.

system_statuses.name: Partial infrastructure visibility for Artifactory ent-las nodes
system_statuses.description: The report states ent-las-07 through ent-las-10 were registered in Datadog with no OS detected and no agent, while ent-las-01 through ent-las-06 had Agent 7.64.1.

root_causes.name: Probable Artifactory LDAP authentication failure caused source verification failure
root_causes.description: Probable root cause: Artifactory ent-las could not sync the Restricted-CRMBuilds-Read-Only LDAP group from qed-ldap.qualcomm.com:636 starting at 06:19:14Z, preventing authorization for CRMBuilds restricted repositories when VerifySrc started 7 seconds later. VerifySrc likely detected missing or inaccessible source content and failed despite exit code 0.

data_gaps.name: VerifySrc step logs unavailable
data_gaps.description: Direct CloudBees step execution logs from VerifySrc on crm-ubuntu327 were not available; the exact error message, such as Artifactory 401/403 or access denied, was not confirmed.

data_gaps.name: Agent infrastructure telemetry unavailable
data_gaps.description: crm-ubuntu327 has no Datadog telemetry coverage, so disk, CPU, memory, and network contribution on the agent cannot be confirmed or excluded.

data_gaps.name: Commander host not identified
data_gaps.description: No Commander host was identified in job metadata, and no Commander-related errors were found during the correlation window.

data_gaps.name: Underlying LDAP failure mode unknown
data_gaps.description: The report recommends checking LDAP server logs, network connectivity, and TLS certificate validity, meaning the precise LDAP-side failure mode was not established in the RCA log.

data_gaps.name: VerifySrc script code issue not fully excluded
data_gaps.description: The RCA states a code-level issue in the VerifySrc script itself cannot be fully excluded without step logs, although it is not the dominant evidenced cause.

preventive_actions.name: Implement LDAP high availability
preventive_actions.description: Implement LDAP failover or redundancy for qed-ldap.qualcomm.com:636 so a single LDAP server failure does not block CRMBuilds jobs across the Artifactory fleet.

preventive_actions.name: Monitor Artifactory LDAP sync failures
preventive_actions.description: Create a Datadog monitor for the Artifactory 'Could not sync ldap groups' log pattern to alert immediately when LDAP sync failures begin.

preventive_actions.name: Configure Artifactory LDAP group caching
preventive_actions.description: Configure Artifactory to cache LDAP group memberships with a reasonable TTL, such as 15–30 minutes, so short LDAP outages do not immediately impact build authorization.

preventive_actions.name: Install Datadog Agent on crm-ubuntu327
preventive_actions.description: Enroll crm-ubuntu327 in Datadog alongside other CRM Ubuntu agents for consistent observability coverage.

preventive_actions.name: Forward CloudBees step logs
preventive_actions.description: Forward CloudBees step logs to Datadog or Splunk to enable centralized RCA without requiring direct host access.

preventive_actions.name: Alert on failed steps with exit code 0
preventive_actions.description: Investigate and alert when CloudBees classifies a step with exit code 0 as failed, and ensure VerifySrc uses non-zero exit codes for failure conditions.

preventive_actions.name: Add pre-build LDAP and Artifactory authorization health check
preventive_actions.description: Add a pre-build step that validates Artifactory connectivity and authorization before VerifySrc, producing a clearer failure when LDAP is unavailable.

remediations.name: Investigate qed-ldap.qualcomm.com:636 availability
remediations.description: Check LDAP server logs, network connectivity, and TLS certificate validity for the LDAPS endpoint at approximately 06:19 UTC to determine why sync requests failed.

remediations.name: Check Artifactory LDAP configuration
remediations.description: Review Artifactory LDAP sync configuration for the Restricted-CRMBuilds-Read-Only group, including bind credentials, connection timeout settings, and failover configuration.

remediations.name: Retrieve VerifySrc step logs
remediations.description: Use CloudBees Commander UI for job f179cba9-dcae-f125-a110-2686f9881e6f and step VerifySrc to view logs and confirm whether output contains Artifactory 401/403 or access denied messages.

remediations.name: Re-run the build after LDAP recovery
remediations.description: If the LDAP server has recovered and Artifactory can authorize CRMBuilds, re-trigger the build; VerifySrc should succeed if authorization is restored.

remediations.name: Verify LDAP server and Artifactory group sync recovery
remediations.description: Confirm qed-ldap.qualcomm.com:636 is operational and all Artifactory ent-las nodes have successfully re-synced Restricted-CRMBuilds-Read-Only before re-running.

step_classes.name: Source verification step
step_classes.description: VerifySrc is described as a source verification step that validates fetched source content before compilation begins.

log_symptoms.name: VerifySrc failed with exit code 0
log_symptoms.description: CloudBees metadata shows failedStep.exitCode = "0" while the step and job outcome were error. The report describes this as an anomalous clean process exit despite failure.

log_symptoms.description: CloudBees executionContext.failedReason was "Compile", described as a broad classification used when source verification fails before compilation begins.

log_symptoms.description: During the VerifySrc step window, network activity was near zero, indicating no active source transfer while the step was running.

system_statuses.name: Infrastructure excluded as cause
system_statuses.description: The RCA concludes infrastructure was definitively excluded because the host was idle and had no CPU, IO, network, memory, disk, or hypervisor contention during the failure window.

system_statuses.name: Zero active incidents during job window
system_statuses.description: Datadog Incidents showed zero active incidents during 06:16:00–06:22:00Z.

root_causes.name: Probable source content validation failure in VerifySrc
root_causes.description: The RCA identifies the probable root cause as a code/data/source-content validation failure. VerifySrc ran on a healthy idle host, exited with code 0, but its output validation logic apparently detected missing, incomplete, inaccessible, checksummed incorrectly, or otherwise invalid source content. Exact validation failure is not confirmed without step logs.

root_causes.name: Candidate invalid or placeholder build number 00000
root_causes.description: The RCA identifies build number "00000" as a strong candidate contributing cause because it may indicate a placeholder or uninitialized build number, potentially meaning the expected source package does not exist in the repository. This is not confirmed in the available evidence.

data_gaps.name: VerifySrc step logs unavailable in Datadog
data_gaps.description: No application-level or step-level logs from crm-ubuntu232 were forwarded to Datadog. The exact VerifySrc validation failure message must be retrieved directly from CloudBees.

data_gaps.name: Exact source validation failure unknown
data_gaps.description: The RCA cannot confirm whether the failure was a missing source package, incomplete package, checksum mismatch, missing required file, repository error response, or invalid build number without VerifySrc stdout/stderr.

data_gaps.name: Specific source repository endpoint unidentified
data_gaps.description: The invoked source repository or artifact server is inferred from pre-step network activity, but the specific endpoint is not identifiable from available metadata.

data_gaps.name: Commander host not identified
data_gaps.description: No Commander host was identified in job metadata, and no Commander-related contribution to the failure was evidenced.

preventive_actions.name: Fix VerifySrc exit code on validation failure
preventive_actions.description: Modify VerifySrc so source validation failures return a non-zero exit code. The RCA identifies exit code 0 on failure as a systemic script design issue affecting at least jobs f179cba9 and f179cb71.

preventive_actions.name: Configure Datadog log pipeline on crm-ubuntu232
preventive_actions.description: Configure log collection for CloudBees agent logs and syslog on crm-ubuntu232 so step-level failure messages are available in Datadog.

preventive_actions.name: Validate build numbers before triggering
preventive_actions.description: Add a pre-flight check in the CloudBees procedure to reject placeholder or invalid build numbers such as "00000" before initiating the build.

preventive_actions.name: Source package pre-validation
preventive_actions.description: Add a pre-step to verify source package existence and integrity in the repository before invoking VerifySrc.

preventive_actions.name: Standardize log forwarding across CRM Ubuntu fleet
preventive_actions.description: Configure fleet-wide log forwarding for crm-ubuntu2xx hosts registered in Datadog to improve RCA coverage.

preventive_actions.name: Add VerifySrc output monitoring
preventive_actions.description: Create a Datadog monitor or CloudBees post-step hook to parse VerifySrc output for validation failure keywords and alert on source validation failures.

remediations.name: Retrieve VerifySrc step logs from CloudBees
remediations.description: Access CloudBees Commander UI for job f179cb71-b909-f19d-9d36-2686f9881e6f, open step VerifySrc, and view the log to obtain the exact validation failure message.

remediations.name: Investigate build number 00000
remediations.description: Verify whether Balsam.LA.2.0.r2-00000-STD.INT-3 uses a placeholder or uninitialized build number and whether the corresponding source package should exist.

remediations.name: Check source repository for Balsam.LA.2.0.r2-00000
remediations.description: Verify that the source package for Balsam.LA.2.0.r2-00000 is present, complete, accessible, and unaffected by recent branch or repository changes.

remediations.name: Verify VerifySrc exit code behavior
remediations.description: Investigate why VerifySrc exits with code 0 on validation failure and correct the behavior if confirmed.

remediations.name: Cross-reference JIRA qstability
remediations.description: Check whether open JIRA tickets for the Balsam component relate to source availability or build failures. The RCA states the observed qstability queries were read-only and out of scope for causality.

It states high confidence that infrastructure is not the cause and high confidence that the failure is in source content validation, but only medium confidence on the specific validation failure.

annotations.name: Cross-job pattern
annotations.description: The RCA notes this is the second analyzed job with VerifySrc failure, exit code 0, and failedReason Compile. Job f179cba9 had an LDAP failure; this job had no LDAP failure detected.

log_record.name: RCA_f179cc58-200a-f193-933e-1ea035bf3243.txt
log_record.description: Root Cause Analysis Report for CloudBees job f179cc58-200a-f193-933e-1ea035bf3243 generated 2026-07-07 by NOVA.

procedures.name: Build.Rel.602829
procedures.description: CloudBees procedure for project CRMBuilds; job CI_SAIL.SI.1.1-18301.1-AU.LEMANS-1_20260706_232312_232318 failed during this procedure.

steps.name: Make
steps.description: Failed CloudBees step with stepId f179cc58-2c18-f12c-85b0-1ea035bf3243. Ran from 06:29:43Z to 09:23:32Z on 2026-07-07 for 2h 53m 49s on qctcrmbld52 and exited with code 4.

step_classes.name: GNU Make compile
step_classes.description: GNU Make-based kernel/firmware compilation for the LEMANS-1 target. The report states exit code 4 means a child process invoked by Make returned a non-zero exit code.

step_classes.name: Device Tree compilation/checking
step_classes.description: DTC and DTB/DTBO checking context for LEMANS-1, including graph_port unit-address warnings and reg_format prerequisite warnings observed in a separate GitHub Actions pipeline.

log_symptoms.name: Make failed with exit code 4
log_symptoms.description: Primary observed failure: the Make step failed at 09:23:32Z with GNU Make exit code 4, classified by the build as Compile failure.

log_symptoms.name: CloudBees job completed_error
log_symptoms.description: Job outcome was completed_error / error and ended at 09:28:52Z.

log_symptoms.name: DTS graph_port unit address warnings
log_symptoms.description: Correlated codebase evidence from Datadog Logs for GitHub Actions: 16 graph_port unit address warnings in arch/arm64/boot/dts/qcom/lemans-evk-camera-sensor.dtsi and arch/arm64/boot/dts/qcom/lemans-camera-sensor.dtsi across four GMSL deserializer nodes. GitHub Actions jobs completed successfully, so this is not direct proof of the CloudBees failure.

log_symptoms.name: DTS reg_format prerequisite warnings
log_symptoms.description: Correlated codebase evidence from GitHub Actions logs: spi_bus_reg, i2c_bus_reg, pci_device_bus_num, and pci_device_reg checks failed prerequisite reg_format in lemans-evk-camx.dtbo and lemans-rtss-mb.dtbo. GitHub Actions jobs completed successfully, so this is not direct proof of the CloudBees failure.

system_statuses.name: Agent host qctcrmbld52 healthy during Make step
system_statuses.description: Datadog metrics showed CPU average 5.3% with peak 14%, memory about 10% of 204 GB, disk flat at 47.1%, and zero I/O wait. The report states no resource exhaustion occurred.

system_statuses.name: qctcrmbld52 Windows Server 2022 with Datadog Agent
system_statuses.description: Agent host qctcrmbld52 was identified as Windows Server 2022 Standard with Datadog Agent v7.52.1 installed; metrics were available.

system_statuses.name: Infrastructure not implicated
system_statuses.description: The RCA classifies Infrastructure Issue: NO. No CPU, memory, disk, network, Datadog incident, or external dependency evidence correlated with the Make failure.

system_statuses.name: QCDFS Kerberos outage not related
system_statuses.description: The report states this job is unrelated to earlier VerifySrc failures and not caused by the QCDFS Kerberos outage; outage ended before the Make failure and the failure mechanism differed.

system_statuses.name: GitHub Actions pipeline succeeded
system_statuses.description: Separate GitHub Actions jobs for related LEMANS-1 targets completed with status success and conclusion success despite DTS warnings.

root_causes.name: Probable code-level compile failure in LEMANS-1 kernel/firmware build
root_causes.description: Probable root cause with MEDIUM confidence: GNU Make exit code 4 indicates a child process such as compiler, linker, assembler, sub-make, or DTC returned non-zero. Host metrics ruled out resource exhaustion, making a code/toolchain failure the most supported explanation.

root_causes.name: Possible DTS structural warning promoted to fatal error
root_causes.description: Inferred candidate cause: CloudBees may have invoked DTC with stricter flags such as -Werror or W=1, promoting known LEMANS-1 DTS graph_port or reg_format warnings to fatal errors. The RCA explicitly notes this is not directly proven because CloudBees Make logs were unavailable.

root_causes.name: Alternative CloudBees-specific AU.LEMANS-1 compile error
root_causes.description: Alternative explanation from the report: the CloudBees AU.LEMANS-1 build may use a different configuration or target variant that exercises a hard compile error not present in the GitHub Actions targets.

data_gaps.name: CloudBees Make step output not examined
data_gaps.description: The exact failing compilation unit cannot be confirmed because direct CloudBees Make step output from qctcrmbld52 was not available in the RCA evidence.

data_gaps.name: No qctcrmbld52 logs in Datadog
data_gaps.description: Datadog returned zero logs for qctcrmbld52 during the Make window because Windows Event Log and application log collection were not configured on the host.

data_gaps.name: Commander host not identified
data_gaps.description: The Commander host was not identified and there was no evidence of Commander-side contribution to the failure.

data_gaps.name: GitHub Actions DTS evidence is out-of-scope for direct execution path
data_gaps.description: DTS warnings were observed in a separate GitHub Actions CI system that succeeded. The RCA uses this only as codebase context and states it does not directly prove the CloudBees Make failure cause.

data_gaps.name: Splunk not queried
data_gaps.description: Splunk was not queried; the report states Datadog provided sufficient evidence for this investigation.

preventive_actions.name: Add DTS lint gate in pre-merge CI
preventive_actions.description: Run make dtbs_check with the same DTC flags used in the CloudBees production build and block merges introducing DTS warnings or errors.

preventive_actions.name: Cross-platform build validation
preventive_actions.description: Ensure pre-merge CI uses the same OS and toolchain as the production CloudBees build environment to catch platform-specific failures early.

preventive_actions.name: Enable Datadog log collection on Windows build agents
preventive_actions.description: Configure Datadog on all qctcrmbld* Windows hosts to collect application logs, CloudBees agent logs, and Windows Event Logs.

preventive_actions.name: Make step timeout and early failure detection
preventive_actions.description: Implement incremental build caching and parallel compilation monitoring to detect failing compilation units earlier and reduce wasted compute.

preventive_actions.name: Build environment parity documentation
preventive_actions.description: Document differences between CloudBees Windows and GitHub Actions Linux environments for LEMANS-1 and maintain a compatibility matrix.

remediations.name: Examine CloudBees Make step logs
remediations.description: Access the Make step output for job f179cc58-200a-f193-933e-1ea035bf3243 and step f179cc58-2c18-f12c-85b0-1ea035bf3243. Search for first occurrences of Error, error:, fatal error:, or *** [ to identify the exact failing compilation unit.

remediations.name: Fix DTS graph_port unit address errors
remediations.description: In lemans-evk-camera-sensor.dtsi and lemans-camera-sensor.dtsi, rename port@2 to port@0 and port@3 to port@1 for all four GMSL deserializer nodes under cci0@ac13000 through cci3@ac16000.

remediations.name: Fix DTS reg_format prerequisite failures
remediations.description: Investigate and fix reg_format prerequisite failures in lemans-evk-camx.dtbo and lemans-rtss-mb.dtbo affecting spi_bus_reg, i2c_bus_reg, pci_device_bus_num, and pci_device_reg checks.

remediations.name: Align DTC flags between CloudBees and GitHub Actions
remediations.description: Compare CloudBees Make DTC invocation flags with the GitHub Actions make -j16 O=temp-out CHECK_DTBS=y command. If CloudBees uses stricter flags, fix DTS issues or align flag sets.

remediations.name: Configure Windows log collection on qctcrmbld52
remediations.description: Configure the Datadog agent on qctcrmbld52 to collect Windows Event Logs and application logs, including CloudBees agent logs.

annotations.name: Confidence MEDIUM
annotations.description: Overall confidence is MEDIUM. Exit code 4 and host health evidence are high-confidence, but the exact failing compilation unit is unknown and DTS causality is inferred.

annotations.name: Last known commit ee11e490952f61d72e5f85d5cb82209c532a88e9
annotations.description: The report identifies commit ee11e490952f61d72e5f85d5cb82209c532a88e9, 'ci: Enable Storage topic branches for pre-merge rootfs generation', dated 2026-07-03. The report states this changed CI branch filtering and is unlikely to be the direct cause.

annotations.name: GitHub Actions evidence scope note
annotations.description: GitHub Actions evidence came from separate independent CI systems building the same LEMANS-1 target. It is codebase context only and not direct CloudBees execution-path evidence.

annotations.name: Infrastructure vs code assessment
annotations.description: RCA assessment: Infrastructure Issue: NO; Code Issue: YES; Mixed: NO.
